mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid improvement in labor market conditions signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, prompting equity investors to price in higher earnings multiples and driving positive short-term market returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.5% within one month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses below the 5.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates robust capacity utilization and construction activity, which historically precedes broad-based earnings upgrades and sustains positive equity momentum over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the denominator for corporate profit margins and reduces recession risk premiums, leading to sustained positive equity returns over a quarterly forecasting window.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns turn negative when real GDP growth exceeds the historical 75th percentile, indicating that growth deceleration rather than absolute growth drives equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: An abrupt tightening of short-term policy rates increases borrowing costs and compresses valuation multiples, causing immediate negative pressure on equity prices over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered episodes, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects consistent labor demand and wage stability, which supports consumer spending and corporate cash flows, yielding positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant difference between months with payroll growth above 150,000 versus months below that threshold over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and stagnant industrial output signals a shift toward services consumption and inventory drawdowns, which historically precedes margin compression and negative equity returns over a quarterly window.
failure_condition: The interaction between retail sales and industrial production fails to produce a negative predictive coefficient for 60-day forward returns in at least 40% of rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production relative to historical trends indicates supply chain bottlenecks or demand destruction, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where industrial production drops below the 110.0 threshold, or the signal exhibits no predictive power beyond a random walk benchmark.
